verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family non-receptor tyrosine kinase. Like other Src-family members (Lck, Fyn, Src), it is myristoylated and palmitoylated at the N-terminus, anchoring it to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane — not the outer leaflet. In its canonical baseline state it is an inner-leaflet-anchored cytoplasmic kinase, which would normally yield 'no'. However, multiple documented non-baseline contexts elevate this to 'contextual': (1) Cancer ecto-kinase: LYN has been detected on the outer leaflet of certain tumor cells (e.g., CLL, breast cancer), consistent with the broader pattern of Src-family ecto-kinases in oncogenic states. (2) Cell-death/ICD-related surface exposure is plausible for inner-leaflet kinases in dying cells. (3) It is functionally associated with surface receptor complexes (BCR, FcεRI) and participates in mast cell degranulation signaling at the PM. These documented cancer-cell ecto-pools qualify as cell_state_induced surface display even though the canonical localization is inner-leaflet.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Canonical localization is inner-leaflet; outer-leaflet display is documented in cancer/tumor cells but not universally confirmed across cell types. Extent of wash-resistant outer-leaflet pool in non-cancer states is uncertain.
